diagrams.description: Public Administration is the academic study and practice of implementing government policy and managing public programs. This flowchart illustrates its systematic approach: the overarching goal is to ensure effective, efficient, and accountable governance. It integrates core functions (planning, organizing, staffing, directing, budgeting—POSDCORB) and principles (public interest, transparency, accountability, rule of law) with major theoretical frameworks—classical bureaucracy, New Public Management, and New Public Service. These guide the policy implementation cycle (formulation → implementation → evaluation → feedback). The resulting insights inform organizational design, human resource management, public budgeting, and performance measurement, ultimately producing public service delivery that builds citizen trust and social equity.
